summary: Performance optimization changes verified. All backend APIs working with caching enabled. Solutions listing returns lightweight projections (48KB vs 462KB), articles listing returns reduced payload (28KB vs 418KB), detail endpoints return full content. Mega menus lazy-load correctly. All 7 main pages load successfully.

test_categories_covered.backend_api: 19/19 passed - health, solutions listing, solution detail, articles, brands, services, projects, mega-menu, news, videos, products, caching, admin login
test_categories_covered.frontend_ui: 7/7 pages loaded - homepage, solutions listing, solution detail (smart-residential-living), blog, services, brands, projects
test_categories_covered.mega_menus: 2/2 working - Solutions mega menu (RESIDENTIAL/COMMERCIAL/SPECIALIZED categories), Services mega menu (DESIGN & PLANNING/CORE SYSTEMS/SUPPORT)

performance_optimizations_verified.server_side_caching: Working - cache.get/cache.set implemented on solutions, articles, brands, services, projects, mega-menu, news, videos, products endpoints
performance_optimizations_verified.lightweight_projections: Working - solutions listing returns only key fields (slug, title, category, badge, etc.), articles listing returns only display fields
performance_optimizations_verified.detail_endpoints: Working - /api/solutions/smart-residential-living returns full document with description and features
performance_optimizations_verified.lazy_loaded_mega_menus: Working - SolutionsMegaMenu, ServicesMegaMenu, IntelligenceMegaMenu, PackagesMegaMenu loaded via dynamic imports
performance_optimizations_verified.lenis_disabled: Verified - SmoothScrollProvider now returns children directly without Lenis wrapper

critical_code_review_comments: Caching implemented correctly with 300-second TTL on most endpoints
Projections correctly exclude heavy fields like full content body from listing endpoints
Detail endpoints correctly return full documents without projection
Mega menus use Next.js dynamic() for lazy loading - reduces initial bundle size
Lenis smooth scroll disabled to improve performance
Source maps disabled in production to reduce network traffic
context_for_next_testing_agent: Performance optimization task complete. Key changes: 1) Server-side caching on all public endpoints (300s TTL), 2) Lightweight projections for listing endpoints (solutions 48KB, articles 28KB), 3) Mega menus lazy-loaded via dynamic imports, 4) Lenis smooth scroll disabled, 5) Source maps disabled. All APIs and pages working correctly.
